Title: Root-specific camalexin biosynthesis controls the plant growth-promoting effects of multiple bacterial strains
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates root-microbe interactions; contributes to defense against pathogens; enables growth-promotion by beneficial rhizobacteria within an optimal concentration range
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes a camalexin-pathway oxidation step similar to CYP71A12/CYP71A13, but exact reaction was not determined in this paper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for accumulation of target product in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly in roots; constitutively expressed in root vasculature/phloem starting in the differentiation zone; weak in hypocotyl; not detected in mature leaves or reproductive organs; expression domain extends closer to root tip after Pseudomonas sp. CH267 or flg22 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root vasculature; bacteria and flg22 responsive spatial pattern in roots
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CYP71A27 native promoter (constitutive/root-specific, bacteria-responsive spatially) for GUS and complementation constructs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas sp. CH267; Pseudomonas simiae WCS417r; Paraburkholderia phytofirmans PsJN; Burkholderia glumae PG1; Serendipita indica; flg22; AgNO3; soil/sand growth system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CYP71A27 reduces root camalexin accumulation, lowers rhizosphere sulfatase activity, abolishes growth-promotion by several beneficial bacteria, increases sensitivity to B. glumae, and decreases S. indica colonization; does not impair leaf camalexin accumulation or leaf pathogen responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate prioritization; T-DNA knockout phenotyping; root camalexin quantification by HPLC/GC-MS; chemical complementation with exogenous camalexin; genetic complementation with native promoter-gene construct; promoter::GUS expression analysis; double-mutant analysis; qPCR; cocultivation with multiple microbes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71A27 was identified by GWAS and mutant validation as a determinant of rhizosphere sulfatase activity. 2. cyp71A27 mutants accumulate less camalexin in roots, especially after elicitation, showing CYP71A27 is part of the camalexin biosynthetic network. 3. Exogenous camalexin restores the mutant defects in sulfatase activity and Pseudomonas-mediated growth promotion, linking CYP71A27 function causally to camalexin. 4. CYP71A27 functions mainly in roots, unlike leaf-associated camalexin biosynthesis roles of other family members. 5. Natural E373Q allelic variation in CYP71A27 correlates with altered camalexin induction/exudation and altered bacterial growth-promotion responses.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using 250k SNP dataset via GWAPP; qPCR-based expression analysis; HPLC/GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising target to tune root camalexin levels and optimize beneficial rhizobacterial interactions, but engineering should avoid both deficiency and excess because the growth-promotion effect depends on a narrow camalexin concentration range; root-specific deployment is preferable
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Both low and high camalexin can disrupt beneficial bacterial growth-promotion; cyp71A27 mutants alter microbial interaction phenotypes; WT plants lose growth-promotion when exogenous camalexin is too high
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Allelic variation E373Q correlates with altered phenotypes, but whether this changes catalytic activity directly remains unresolved; CYP71A27 affects root but not leaf camalexin biology

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolite that also contributes to beneficial root-microbe interactions
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: camalexin biosynthesis module in roots and shoots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Camalexin-pathway oxidation step; exact reaction not assigned in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not constitutively expressed in roots; strongly induced by flg22 in root cortex cells; induced by Pseudomonas sp. CH267 in roots
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CYP71A12 promoter used in GUS reporter line (inducible)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flg22; Pseudomonas sp. CH267; soil growth; AgNO3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: cyp71A12 mutants show reduced root camalexin, lower sulfatase activity, and loss of Pseudomonas CH267-mediated growth-promotion; among single mutants it had the lowest flg22-induced root camalexin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; root camalexin quantification; promoter::GUS; double-mutant analysis; cocultivation assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71A12 is a major contributor to flg22-induced camalexin accumulation in roots. 2. Loss of CYP71A12 reduces sulfatase activity and abolishes bacterial growth-promotion similar to cyp71A27. 3. Double-mutant analysis indicates additive contribution with CYP71A27. 4. Exogenous camalexin did not rescue cyp71A12 growth-promotion defect, suggesting possible additional function beyond simply providing camalexin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modulating camalexin-mediated root interactions, but engineering may be complex because its loss was not chemically complemented by camalexin and may affect additional signaling functions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption abolishes beneficial bacterial growth-promotion and lowers camalexin; may have additional signaling-related effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Known camalexin gene used as benchmark control; stronger role in flg22-induced root camalexin than CYP71A13 in this study

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolite contributing to pathogen resistance and root-microbe interactions
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: camalexin biosynthesis module in shoots and roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a camalexin-pathway oxidation step; exact step not reassigned here
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots by Pseudomonas sp. CH267; known major contributor to shoot camalexin, while contributing less strongly to root camalexin than CYP71A12 in this study
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas sp. CH267; flg22; AgNO3; Botrytis cinerea; Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: cyp71A13 mutants reduce sulfatase activity and bacterial growth-promotion, lower root camalexin, impair leaf camalexin accumulation and increase susceptibility to Botrytis cinerea
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; pathogen assays; root and leaf camalexin quantification; double-mutant analysis; chemical complementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71A13 contributes to camalexin accumulation in roots and strongly to leaves. 2. cyp71A13 mutants lose Pseudomonas-mediated growth-promotion and show reduced sulfatase activity. 3. Exogenous camalexin rescues the cyp71A13 growth-promotion defect. 4. Unlike CYP71A27, CYP71A13 is important for leaf camalexin accumulation and Botrytis resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key camalexin engineering target affecting both root microbiome interaction and leaf pathogen defense, but manipulation may incur defense-growth tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced pathogen resistance in leaves when disrupted; altered beneficial microbe interactions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as known camalexin-pathway control; root role is weaker than CYP71A12 under flg22 in this study

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Putative contributor to root-associated camalexin-mediated microbial interaction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted camalexin-pathway oxidation step; exact reaction unknown
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low expression compared with other CYP71A genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: cyp71A28 mutants show lower sulfatase activity, reduced root camalexin, and loss of Pseudomonas CH267-mediated growth-promotion, but evidence for direct pathway role is weaker than for CYP71A27
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; root camalexin quantification; expression analysis of pathway genes; cocultivation assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cyp71A28 phenocopies other CYP71A mutants in sulfatase activity, root camalexin reduction, and bacterial growth-promotion loss. 2. Low expression and inability to amplify a full coding region prevented firm functional assignment. 3. The paper treats CYP71A28 as a possible but not fully resolved component of the camalexin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently low-confidence engineering target pending biochemical confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Uncertain due to unresolved gene structure/expression and function
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors explicitly state conclusions about CYP71A28 involvement remain uncertain

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolite and rhizosphere interaction modulator
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a late step required for camalexin biosynthesis; exact reaction not described in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: pad3 mutant has reduced rhizosphere sulfatase activity, and exogenous camalexin restores this phenotype to WT level
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant sulfatase assay; chemical complementation with camalexin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A known camalexin-deficient mutant, pad3, showed lower soil sulfatase activity. 2. Exogenous camalexin restored the sulfatase phenotype, supporting the causal role of camalexin rather than a CYP71A27-specific nonmetabolic effect.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Confirms late-step camalexin biosynthesis as a lever to modulate root microbiome function, but likely with defense-related tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing PAD3 would impair camalexin-dependent defense and beneficial interaction tuning
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as an independent camalexin-pathway control supporting metabolite causality
